title: Integrating large language model-based agents into a virtual patient chatbot for clinical anamnesis training
citation: Laverde N, Grevisse C, Jaramillo S, Manrique R. Integrating large language model-based agents into a virtual patient chatbot for clinical anamnesis training. Computational and Structural Biotechnology Journal. 2025;27:2481-2491. doi:10.1016/j.csbj.2025.05.025. Accessed 2026-05-03.
permitted_uses: Architecture context for LLM-based virtual patient agents using memory, retrieval, cognitive mechanisms, and teacher-authored cases.
Research context for asynchronous clinical history-taking practice.
claims_supported: The article describes LLM-based generative agents for virtual patient history-taking practice.
The article reports use of memory/retrieval/cognitive mechanisms and teacher-created cases.
The article highlights plausible, context-aware responses and usability evaluation.
claims_not_supported: That LLM agents are safe for unsupervised high-stakes scoring.
That XR multimodal encounters are validated by the article.
